title: Masterclass in Endometriosis & Surgery
description:
Take control of your endometriosis journey with this comprehensive masterclass led by Dr. Naomi Whittaker, a NaProTechnology trained OBGYN and endometriosis specialist.

Designed for both women living with endometriosis and healthcare professionals, this course offers:

• Clarity and understanding — Debunk common myths and gain in-depth knowledge about endometriosis and its impact on fertility.

• Surgical insights — Explore various surgical approaches, including NaProTechnology's meticulous techniques and focus on adhesion prevention.

• Informed decision-making — Learn how to choose the right endometriosis surgeon for you and navigate the surgical process with confidence.

• Long-term management — Discover effective strategies for managing endometriosis symptoms through lifestyle changes, supplements, and medications.

Whether you're considering surgery, waiting for an appointment, or seeking to better guide your patients, this masterclass empowers you with the knowledge and tools you need to find lasting relief and achieve your fertility goals.
shortDescription: Comprehensive guide for patients preparing for endometriosis surgery. Covers the entire surgical journey, including how to choose a surgeon, and automatically includes the Long-Term Management course as a bonus.
imageAlt: a woman sits in front of a microphone and a laptop

seo.title: Endometriosis Surgery Masterclass | RRM Academy
seo.description: Prepare for endometriosis surgery with this comprehensive course from Dr. Naomi Whittaker, MD. 39 videos, 2h 53m, self-paced. Includes long-term management. $199.

steps.title: Introduction
steps.content:
Complete this course with an 80% or better on the post-class quiz to receive a printable certificate for the completion of Dr. Whittaker's Masterclass in Endometriosis and Surgery, as well as the Endometriosis & Surgery badge and the Endometriosis Management badge.

Hi.

Dr. Whittaker here.

Thank you for joining me! I hope you're as excited to be a part of this as I am.

This masterclass will continue to be updated with new and relevant information.

steps.title: OBGYN Subspecialties, Their Focus & How It Affects Endo Care
How Creighton Biomarkers Help with Surgical Planning

steps.title: Workbook: Insider Guide to Choosing an Endometriosis Surgeon
steps.content:
Download your copy of the Insider's Guide to Choosing an Endometriosis Surgeon by Naomi Whittaker, MD.

This workbook covers:

  • Non-surgical management approaches (basic vs. advanced)
  • Theories of origin
  • Diagnosis strategies
  • Treatment approaches
  • The role of hysterectomy
  • Surgical volume considerations
  • Questions to ask your potential surgeon

Download Workbook (PDF)

steps.title: Share Feedback
steps.content:
If you share a public or anonymous testimonial about the class it will help me a lot!

  1. You can make a public IG post
  2. Or share an anonymous testimonial. DM me @napro_fertility_surgeon on IG and I will keep your testimonial anonymous.
  3. Or you can record a video and upload it to this Google form.

faqs.question: Who is this course for?
faqs.answer: This course is designed for patients preparing for endometriosis surgery or considering it. It's also valuable for women recently diagnosed with endometriosis who want to understand all treatment options, and for partners or family members who want to support someone through the surgical journey.

faqs.question: Do I need medical training to take this course?
faqs.answer: No. This course is written for patients, not clinicians. Dr. Whittaker explains medical concepts in plain language with visual aids. Healthcare professionals will also find it valuable for understanding the patient perspective.

faqs.question: What will I be able to do after this course?
faqs.answer: You'll know how to evaluate and choose an endometriosis surgeon, understand what to expect before, during, and after surgery, know the right questions to ask your doctor, and have a long-term management plan for living well with endometriosis.

faqs.question: Is this course accredited for CME credits?
faqs.answer: This course is not currently CME-accredited. It is designed as patient education. Healthcare professionals seeking CME should check the IIRRM and FACTS About Fertility course catalogs for accredited options.

faqs.question: How long do I have access to the course?
faqs.answer: You have one year of access from the date of purchase. This gives you time to revisit materials as you prepare for and recover from surgery.

faqs.question: Does the course include the Long-Term Management course?
faqs.answer: Yes. When you purchase the Masterclass in Endometriosis & Surgery, the Long-Term Endometriosis Management course ($19 value) is automatically included as a bonus.

faqs.question: Can I get a scholarship?
faqs.answer: As a 501(c)(3) nonprofit, RRM Academy offers a limited number of scholarships for paid courses. Contact us to request a scholarship application.

faqs.question: What topics does the curriculum cover?
faqs.answer: The course has 9 sections and 46 lessons covering: introduction to endometriosis, choosing a surgeon, surgical preparation, what happens during surgery, recovery, long-term management (diet, lifestyle, supplements, medications), and when to seek additional care.

title: Long Term Endometriosis Management
description:
This essential course equips both women with endometriosis and healthcare professionals with the knowledge and tools for effective long-term symptom management.

For Patients:
• Empowerment through Knowledge — Gain a deep understanding of how lifestyle, diet, supplements, and medications can influence your endometriosis symptoms.
• Practical Strategies — Discover actionable steps to implement in your daily life for improved well-being and reduced pain.
• Holistic Approach — Explore a range of evidence-based and complementary therapies to manage endometriosis holistically.

For Healthcare Professionals:
• Expand Your Expertise — Enhance your understanding of long-term endometriosis management strategies beyond surgical interventions.
• Guide Your Patients — Gain valuable insights to confidently advise and support your patients in managing their endometriosis symptoms.
• Improve Patient Outcomes — Discover effective tools and resources to help your patients achieve lasting relief and improve their quality of life.
shortDescription: Non-surgical wellness strategies for endometriosis: diet, lifestyle, supplements, and medications. Can be purchased separately or included with the Masterclass.

seo.title: Long-Term Endometriosis Management Course | RRM Academy
seo.description: Non-surgical strategies for endometriosis: diet, lifestyle, supplements, and medications. Taught by Dr. Naomi Whittaker, MD. 7 videos, 15 minutes. $19.

steps.title: References
steps.content: References for the Long Term Endometriosis Management course are cited within each video lesson. Key topics and their supporting literature are discussed in context throughout the course.

steps.title: Endo Functional Living Guide
steps.content:
Download the Empowered Living with Endometriosis guide by the RESTORE Center and Dr. Patrick Yeung Jr.

This comprehensive guide covers:

  • Endometriosis & Functional Medicine
  • Menstrual Cycle Phases
  • Eating for Your Cycle
  • Building a Healthy Plate
  • Chemicals & Toxins to Avoid
  • Bristol Stool Chart
  • Holistic Self-Care
  • Daily Wellness Tips

Download Guide (PDF)

faqs.question: Who is this course for?
faqs.answer: Women living with endometriosis who want non-surgical strategies for managing symptoms. Ideal for post-surgical patients or those not yet ready for surgery.

faqs.question: What topics are covered?
faqs.answer: Diet and nutrition for endometriosis, lifestyle modifications, evidence-based supplements, medications that support long-term management, and when to consider further intervention.

faqs.question: How is this different from the Masterclass?
faqs.answer: The Masterclass covers the full surgical journey (46 lessons, 2h 53m) and includes this course as a bonus. This standalone course (7 videos, 15 minutes) focuses specifically on non-surgical management.

faqs.question: Is this course free?
faqs.answer: No, it's $19. RRM Academy offers several free courses if you'd like to start there. Scholarships are available for paid courses.

faqs.question: How long do I have access?
faqs.answer: One year from purchase date, or lifetime if you purchased the Masterclass (which includes this course).

title: Postpartum Depression & Anxiety: A Restorative Approach to Recovery
description:
This course discusses postpartum depression and anxiety, exploring its causes, symptoms, and a restorative approach to healing.

Dr. Naomi Whittaker, a fertility surgeon specializing in women's health, delves into the limitations of conventional treatments and offers insights into the effectiveness of bioidentical progesterone.

Key topics covered:
• Understanding the different types of postpartum mood disorders
• Exploring the physiological and hormonal factors contributing to postpartum depression
• Discussing the limitations of traditional treatment approaches like SSRIs
• Highlighting the benefits of bioidentical progesterone as a natural and effective solution
• Providing actionable steps for prevention and seeking support
shortDescription: Explore postpartum depression and anxiety, its causes and symptoms, and a restorative approach to healing with bioidentical progesterone.

seo.title: Postpartum Depression & Anxiety: A Restorative Approach | RRM Academy
seo.description: Learn about postpartum depression and anxiety, its causes, and a restorative approach to recovery using bioidentical progesterone.

faqs.question: Is this course really free?
faqs.answer: Yes, 100% free. RRM Academy is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit. Free courses are part of our mission to make restorative reproductive medicine education accessible to everyone.

faqs.question: Do I need to create an account?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free account is required to track your progress and provide access to course materials including downloadable resources and transcripts.

faqs.question: How long do I have access?
faqs.answer: Lifetime access. You can revisit the materials anytime.

faqs.question: What format are the lessons?
faqs.answer: Self-paced video lectures with downloadable resources and transcripts. You can learn on your own schedule.

title: Restorative Reproductive Medicine (RRM) vs Standard ART: A New Approach to Infertility
description:
This course dives deep into the world of infertility treatment, comparing the standard Assisted Reproductive Technology (ART) like IVF and IUI with a lesser-known approach called Restorative Reproductive Medicine (RRM).

Dr. Naomi Whittaker, an OBGYN fertility surgeon specializing in RRM, discusses the limitations of conventional infertility treatment and explores the potential of RRM as a more holistic and effective solution.

Key topics covered:
• The shortcomings of standard OBGYN training in addressing infertility
• The facts behind IVF and IUI success rates and their associated risks
• The ethical, financial, and medical concerns surrounding ART
• The principles of RRM and its focus on identifying and treating the root causes of infertility
• Real patient stories showcasing the effectiveness of RRM
shortDescription: Compare standard ART (IVF/IUI) with Restorative Reproductive Medicine, exploring root-cause approaches to infertility with real patient stories.

seo.title: RRM vs IVF: Comparing Fertility Treatments | RRM Academy
seo.description: Understand how Restorative Reproductive Medicine compares to IVF and other assisted reproductive technologies. Evidence-based, taught by a board-certified OBGYN.

sections.title: Lesson 1. A New Approach to Infertility
Lesson 3. Questioning the Evidence for IUI & IVF

faqs.question: Who is this course for?
faqs.answer: Anyone considering fertility treatment who wants to understand all options. This includes patients weighing IVF against restorative approaches, healthcare providers who want to counsel patients about alternatives, and couples at the beginning of their fertility journey.

faqs.question: Does this course tell me IVF is wrong?
faqs.answer: No. This course presents both approaches with their evidence, outcomes, costs, and best-use scenarios. The goal is informed decision-making, not prescribing one path. Some patients benefit most from RRM, some from IVF, and some from a combination.

faqs.question: What data sources does this course use?
faqs.answer: The course draws on peer-reviewed research published in journals including Fertility and Sterility, BMC Pregnancy and Childbirth, and the Journal of Restorative Reproductive Medicine. All claims are cited.

faqs.question: Will this help me decide between RRM and IVF?
faqs.answer: This course gives you the information to make that decision with your doctor. It covers: how each approach works, published outcomes by condition, cost comparison, what to expect from each treatment path, and questions to ask your provider.

faqs.question: Is this course really free?
faqs.answer: Yes, 100% free. RRM Academy is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit. Free courses are part of our mission to make restorative reproductive medicine education accessible to everyone.

faqs.question: Do I need to create an account?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free account is required to track your progress and provide access to course materials including downloadable resources and transcripts.

faqs.question: How long do I have access?
faqs.answer: Lifetime access. You can revisit the materials anytime.

faqs.question: What format are the lessons?
faqs.answer: Self-paced video lectures with downloadable resources and transcripts. You can learn on your own schedule.

title: Hormones Through the Lifespan: Cycles, Conditions, and Menopause
description: Dr. Ana Garcia, OB/GYN and NaProTechnology-trained physician, walks through the full arc of female hormonal health: how cycles work, what disrupts them, conditions like PCOS and endometriosis, and how to support the body through perimenopause and menopause with a restorative approach.
shortDescription: A comprehensive overview of hormonal health across a woman's life — menstrual cycles, common conditions, and the transition to menopause — presented by Dr. Ana Garcia, OB/GYN.

seo.title: Hormones Through the Lifespan | RRM Academy
seo.description: Learn how hormones change across a woman's life — cycles, conditions, and menopause — with Dr. Ana Garcia, OB/GYN.

title: Pelvic Floor Rehabilitation
description: Dr. Amanda Simmons, Doctor of Physical Therapy and pelvic floor specialist at Ascend Pelvic Health, covers the anatomy and function of the pelvic floor, common dysfunctions (pain, prolapse, incontinence), and evidence-based rehabilitation approaches for patients navigating reproductive and pelvic health conditions.
shortDescription: Understanding and rehabilitating the pelvic floor — pain, dysfunction, and recovery — with Dr. Amanda Simmons, Physical Therapist at Ascend Pelvic Health.

seo.title: Pelvic Floor Rehabilitation | RRM Academy
seo.description: Learn about pelvic floor dysfunction and evidence-based rehabilitation from Dr. Amanda Simmons, pelvic floor physical therapist.

title: Infertility as Existential Trauma
description: Dr. Clay Brigance, Licensed Professional Counselor and researcher, examines infertility as a form of existential trauma and PTSD. This session covers the psychological impact of fertility struggles, grief responses, and therapeutic approaches that help patients process and heal — essential knowledge for both patients and the providers who support them.
shortDescription: The psychological and existential toll of infertility — trauma, grief, and PTSD — explored through a clinical lens by Dr. Clay Brigance, LPC.

seo.title: Infertility as Existential Trauma | RRM Academy
seo.description: Understand the psychological and existential dimensions of infertility with Dr. Clay Brigance, LPC and researcher.

title: Fertility-Based Methods of Family Planning: New Horizons
description: Dr. William Nolan presents the current landscape of fertility-based methods of family planning (FBMFPs), including the latest research, emerging methods, and how these approaches align with NaProTechnology and restorative reproductive medicine principles. Designed for both providers and patients seeking alternatives to hormonal contraception.
shortDescription: An evidence-based overview of fertility awareness and NaProTechnology-aligned family planning methods, presented by Dr. William Nolan.

seo.title: Fertility-Based Methods of Family Planning | RRM Academy
seo.description: Explore evidence-based fertility awareness and family planning methods with Dr. William Nolan.

title: Why the AIP Diet Is Not Enough to Reduce Inflammation
description: Rita Johnson, functional medicine practitioner, breaks down the science and limitations of the Autoimmune Protocol (AIP) diet for patients managing chronic inflammation, endometriosis, PCOS, and other reproductive conditions. Learn what diet alone cannot address, and what a comprehensive anti-inflammatory protocol — including gut health, lifestyle, and targeted support — actually requires.
shortDescription: Functional medicine practitioner Rita Johnson explains why the Autoimmune Protocol diet alone falls short — and what a complete anti-inflammatory strategy actually looks like.

seo.title: Why the AIP Diet Is Not Enough | RRM Academy
seo.description: Learn why the AIP diet alone isn't enough to reduce inflammation — and what a complete anti-inflammatory strategy looks like.

title: Functional Lab Testing, NaPro Technology, and Preconception Care
description: Dr. Ana Garcia (OB/GYN), Hannah Ducote (RN, fertility coach), and Elena Climov (FNP-C, IFMCP) discuss how functional lab testing integrates with NaProTechnology and RRM protocols to support preconception care. Topics include hormone panels, thyroid, micronutrient testing, and how to use data to guide targeted preconception support.
shortDescription: A panel discussion on functional lab testing, NaProTechnology protocols, and preconception care with Dr. Ana Garcia, Hannah Ducote, and Elena Climov.

seo.title: Functional Lab Testing, NaPro Technology & Preconception Care | RRM Academy
seo.description: Panel discussion on functional lab testing and NaProTechnology-aligned preconception protocols with Dr. Ana Garcia, Hannah Ducote, and Elena Climov.
